descriptions: Pelle-Gambazza recognized Luigi Mancuso as Capo-Crimine, indicating a hierarchical relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: Luigi avesse il ruolo di Capo-Crimine riconosciuto dai Pelle-Gambazza di San Luca

descriptions: IS lists Abu al-Fadl al-Abbas Forces as a non-state opponent, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Other non-state opponents: ... Abu al-Fadl al-Abbas Forces

descriptions: Gente Nueva de Los Salgueiro is a faction opposed by the original Gente Nueva.
edges.evidence_quote: la Gente Nueva original se mantuvo liderando a La Gente Nueva en Agua Prieta apoyando a otras facciones como Gente Nueva Salazar, Gente Nueva del Tigre, y Gente Nueva del Jaguar Arvizu, contra La Línea y contra la Gente Nueva de Los Salgueiro

descriptions: Gente Nueva de Los Salgueiro is also against La Línea, but the text groups them with the opposition.
edges.evidence_quote: la Gente Nueva original se mantuvo liderando a La Gente Nueva en Agua Prieta apoyando a otras facciones como Gente Nueva Salazar, Gente Nueva del Tigre, y Gente Nueva del Jaguar Arvizu, contra La Línea y contra la Gente Nueva de Los Salgueiro

Zone 2.description: Zone 2 is a British rap group and street gang based in Peckham, London, active since 2016. They have gained millions of views and streams through their music on YouTube and streaming platforms. The group is known for its rivalry with Moscow17, which has resulted in stabbings and murders. Members have been involved in legal issues including drug supply and knife possession.

descriptions: Zone 2 and Moscow17 are rivals, with a history of violence including stabbings and murders.
edges.evidence_quote: Zone 2 is noteworthy for its rivalry with Moscow17.

Cedar Block Piru.description: The Cedar Block Piru is a set of the Piru gang alliance, part of the larger Bloods gang alliance, formed around 1980 in the west side of Compton, California. It gained notability for its connection to rapper The Game, who has referenced the gang in his songs. The gang was involved in the 1992 murder of Allen King and his three children. Its territory includes multiple cities across the United States.

descriptions: Campanella Park Piru is listed as an ally of Cedar Block Piru.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Fruit Town Piru , West Side Piru , Tree Top Piru , Campanella Park Piru

descriptions: Fruit Town Piru is listed as an ally of Cedar Block Piru.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Fruit Town Piru , West Side Piru , Tree Top Piru , Campanella Park Piru

descriptions: Mob Piru is listed as a rival of Cedar Block Piru.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Mob Piru , Palmer Blocc Compton Crips

descriptions: Palmer Blocc Compton Crips is listed as a rival of Cedar Block Piru.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Mob Piru , Palmer Blocc Compton Crips

descriptions: Tree Top Piru is listed as an ally of Cedar Block Piru.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Fruit Town Piru , West Side Piru , Tree Top Piru , Campanella Park Piru

descriptions: West Side Piru is listed as an ally of Cedar Block Piru.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Fruit Town Piru , West Side Piru , Tree Top Piru , Campanella Park Piru

descriptions: Cedar Block Piru is listed as an ally of Elm Street Piru.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Cedar Block Piru , Fruit Town Piru , Tree Top Piru

descriptions: Cedar Block Piru is an ally of Fruit Town Piru.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Cedar Block Piru

Fruit Belt Gang.description: Street gang based in Buffalo, New York, founded in the 1960s-1970s in the Fruit Belt area. Primarily African American, it engages in drug trafficking, weapons smuggling, and murder. The gang is in direct opposition to the Central Park Gang and has been involved in ongoing violent conflicts.

descriptions: The Fruit Belt Gang is in direct opposition to the Central Park Gang, indicating a rivalry.
edges.evidence_quote: Fruit Belt Gang also known as the Fruit Belt Posse , is a street gang based in Buffalo, New York in the Fruit Belt area and is the direct opposition to the Central Park Gang.

descriptions: Violence and shootings occur between the two gangs.
edges.evidence_quote: After the establishment of the Central Park Gangs, violence has been rampant between the two gangs in the area of Fruit Belt and Cold Springs and many shootings in the area are due to the violence between the two gangs.

descriptions: Central Park Gang members shot Fruit Belt Gang members in retaliation for a murder.
edges.evidence_quote: On July 4, 2020, members of the Fruit Belt Gang, Antwaine Parker and Michael Woods, were shot by Central Park Gang members and transported to a hospital where Woods threw a gun away at the scene before being transported which caused both members to face illegal gun possession charges which they both pleaded guilty to in September of the same year. The shooting was in retaliation for the murder of Central Park Gang member Shariff Jackson by the Fruit Belt Gang.

descriptions: Cheyon Evans, associated with 37/OJB, was killed by a 67 member, indicating rivalry.
edges.evidence_quote: Cheyon Evans was known as 'Stompz' and allegedly was a part of the 37/OJB gang, with him also having ties to the CT gang based in Clapham, one of 67's main rivals.

descriptions: Crazy Dragon Killers kidnapped and assaulted White Boy Posse members, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang first received media attention in Edmonton in 2004 after five White Boy Posse members were kidnapped and assaulted by rival gang the Crazy Dragon Killers.

White Uno Gang.description: Italian criminal organization active from 1987 to 1994, primarily in Emilia-Romagna and Marche. Composed largely of police officers with right-wing extremist ties, the gang committed 102 heists, resulting in 24 deaths and 102 injuries. Founded by the Savi brothers, it was known for using a white Fiat Uno. The gang was dismantled in 1994 after police investigations.

descriptions: Falange Armata claimed responsibility for a massacre committed by the White Uno Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: The massacre was immediately claimed by the terrorist group Falange Armata.

descriptions: Peckwater is listed as a rival of Mali Boys in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Beaumont Crew / Let’s Get Rich (LGR), Priory Court (PC) / Higham Hill, CGM, Anyone Can Go (ACG), Custom House (CH), Peckwater (51st)

edges.source: Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine
descriptions: Abu Ali Mustafa Brigades is the paramilitary wing of PFLP.
edges.evidence_quote: The military wing of the PFLP is called the Abu Ali Mustafa Brigades.

edges.source: Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine
descriptions: Abu Ali Mustafa Brigades is the military wing of the PFLP.
edges.evidence_quote: The military wing of the PFLP is called the Abu Ali Mustafa Brigades.

descriptions: Enemy listed in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: Cobras españolas

descriptions: Tambovskaya Bratva believed Velikie Luki were responsible for an assassination attempt on Kumarin, leading to retaliation.
edges.evidence_quote: He believed that the Velikie Luki were responsible and, later, Viktor Gavrilenkov and Nikolai Gavrilenkov were shot with Viktor Gavrilenkov being killed in the bedlam.

descriptions: COM is a dissident faction from B40 and PCM.
edges.evidence_quote: Isto fez surgir outra facção Comando Organizado do Maranhão (COM), uma dissidência das duas facções maranhenses

descriptions: The founder of Cártel de Tláhuac previously worked for Los Caletri.
edges.evidence_quote: el primer jefe del grupo Felipe de Jesús Pérez Luna, alias el Ojos, se inició trabajando para la banda de secuestradores Los Caletri

descriptions: IS-TP is an official affiliate of IS.
edges.evidence_quote: Islamic State – Turkey Province (IS-TP) was formed in 2019 and is active in Turkey.

descriptions: The Grey Wolves list Alperen Hearths as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Alperen Hearths

descriptions: The Vietnamese Flying Dragons were a former branch of the Flying Dragons.
edges.evidence_quote: The Vietnamese Flying Dragons were a former branch of the Flying Dragons gang that consisted of primarily Vietnamese members.

Minore Mafia clan.description: Historical Sicilian Mafia clan originating from Castellammare del Golfo, founded in the 1910s. It led the Trapani Mafia family from the 1950s and controlled the mandamento of Trapani until a conflict with the Corleonesi led to the assassination of boss Totò Minore in 1982 and the clan's decline. The clan was involved in narcotics trafficking and money laundering.

descriptions: Minore clan connected to American Mafia through Salvatore Maranzano.
edges.evidence_quote: Salvatore Maranzano, who would later emigrate to the United States and become an important figure within the American Mafia

descriptions: Minore clan formed a strong alliance with Buccellato clan.
edges.evidence_quote: The Minore clan went on to form a very strong alliance with the Buccellato Mafia clan

descriptions: Minore clan had close ties with Catania Mafia family.
edges.evidence_quote: Totò Minore developed very close ties to the Catania Mafia family

descriptions: Minore clan had a falling out with Corleonesi, leading to assassination of Totò Minore.
edges.evidence_quote: Their leadership lasted until a falling out with the Corleonesi

descriptions: Corleonesi initially backed Minore clan against Rimi and Badalamenti.
edges.evidence_quote: The emerging Corleonesi Mafia clan at this point backed the Minores and Buccellatos against the Rimis and Badalamentis

descriptions: Minore clan also had rivalry with the Mafia family of Cinisi, related to Rimi.
edges.evidence_quote: in turn with the Mafia family of Cinisi headed by Gaetano Badalamenti

descriptions: Minore clan had a fierce rivalry with Rimi clan in the 1960s-1970s.
edges.evidence_quote: Both the Minore and Buccellato clan developed a fierce rivalry with the Rimi Mafia clan

descriptions: Minore clan led the Trapani Mafia family from the 1950s.
edges.evidence_quote: From the 1950s, the Minore clan led the Trapani Mafia family

descriptions: ETA exchanged weapons and provided logistical support to FP-25.
edges.evidence_quote: ETA exchanged weapons, explosives and provided logistical support to the Forças Populares 25 de Abril (FP-25), a Portuguese far-left terrorist group.

descriptions: Barriox13 has an alliance with Gardena 13.
edges.evidence_quote: It has also clashed with black gangs such as the Campanella Park Piru, 135th St Piru, and West Side Piru, although it has an alliance with Gardena 13 and the Mexican Mafia.

descriptions: Anyone Can Go is listed as a rival of Mali Boys in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Beaumont Crew / Let’s Get Rich (LGR), Priory Court (PC) / Higham Hill, CGM, Anyone Can Go (ACG), Custom House (CH), Peckwater (51st)

Kyokuryū-kai.description: The Kyokuryū-kai is a yakuza criminal organization based on the Okinawa island of Japan, with an estimated membership of 210–270. It was formed in 1970 as the Okinawa-rengo Kyokuryu-kai by two gangs, the Naha Clan and the Koza Clan. It was Okinawa's largest organized crime group from 1983 until 1990 when a splinter group, the Okinawa Kyokuryu-kai, broke away. The group has been in conflict with the Yamaguchi-gumi and its official policy forbids drug trafficking.

descriptions: Koza Clan merged with Naha Clan to form Kyokuryu-kai in 1970.
edges.evidence_quote: The Kyokuryu-kai was formed in 1970 as the Okinawa-rengo Kyokuryu-kai by two gangs, the Naha Clan and the Koza Clan.

descriptions: Naha Clan merged with Koza Clan to form Kyokuryu-kai in 1970.
edges.evidence_quote: The Kyokuryu-kai was formed in 1970 as the Okinawa-rengo Kyokuryu-kai by two gangs, the Naha Clan and the Koza Clan.

descriptions: Kyokuryu-kai and Okinawa Kyokuryu-kai were in conflict for years after the split.
edges.evidence_quote: The Kyokuryu-kai had been in conflict with the Okinawa Kyokuryu-kai for years

descriptions: Okinawa Kyokuryu-kai is a splinter group that broke away from Kyokuryu-kai in 1990.
edges.evidence_quote: the Kyokuryu-kai is one of the two designated yakuza groups in Okinawa Prefecture along with its splinter group Okinawa Kyokuryu-kai

descriptions: Kyokuryu-kai and Yamaguchi-gumi were in a violent conflict from 1973 to 1981.
edges.evidence_quote: From 1973 to 1981, the Kyokuryu-kai was in fierce conflict with the Yamaguchi-gumi

descriptions: Okinawa Kyokuryu-kai split from Kyokuryu-kai in 1990, making it a splinter group.
edges.evidence_quote: The Okinawa Kyokuryu-kai was formed in 1990 when it split from Okinawa's main yakuza group, the Kyokuryu-kai.

descriptions: Kyokuryu-kai attacks and expels yakuza syndicates entering Okinawa, including Toa-kai.
edges.evidence_quote: Okinawa's underworld has been known for its exclusiveness since the 20th century, where the dominating Kyokuryu-kai has persistently attacked and violently expelled any yakuza syndicate attempting to enter the island.

descriptions: Bandidos are in conflict with Freeway Riders.
edges.evidence_quote: Five members of the Hagen Bandidos chapter as well as the president of the Iron Bloods 58, a Bandidos support club, were charged in August 2019 with committing violent crimes during a conflict with the rival Freeway Riders Motorcycle Club.

Tambovskaya Bratva.description: Russian criminal organization based in Saint Petersburg, founded in the 1980s by Vladimir Kumarin and Valery Ledovskikh. Initially recruited only from the Tambov region, engaged in racketeering and later expanded across northwestern Russia. Involved in violent conflicts with rival gangs and later legalized earnings through fuel company PTK.

descriptions: Tambov gang leader Kumarin was connected to Cali Cartel via money-laundering scheme.
edges.evidence_quote: the German firm St. Petersburg Real Estate Holding Company ... had fallen under suspicion of being involved in a money-laundering scheme through the Cali cocaine cartel with connections to Kumarin.

descriptions: A member of the Chechen mafia introduced Tambovskaya Bratva associates to Japanese yakuza.
edges.evidence_quote: Temo Davidov, a member of the Chechen mafia which had caused Manvel to flee the Soviet Union for Stockholm where Kinichi Kamiyasu gave him a job and a Polish passport, introduced the Russian mafia, Petrov, Kuzmin, and Malyshev, to the son of a Japanese yakuza from Kobe-Osaka, Kinichi Kamiyasu.

descriptions: Tambovskaya Bratva fought the Kazan Gang for control of St. Petersburg's energy trade in 1993-1994.
edges.evidence_quote: During the later half of 1993 and into 1994 and with bank accounts in New York, Vladimir I. Dyakov of the Kazan Gang, which had many very devout Muslim Tatars, fought the Tambov Gang for control of St. Petersburg's energy trade.

descriptions: Tambov gang engaged in a firefight with Malyshevskaya.
edges.evidence_quote: multiple members of Kumarin's gang were convicted for the bloody firefight with another criminal gang, Malyshevskaya.

descriptions: Tambovskaya Bratva fought Malyshevskaya OGG in a violent conflict in 1989.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1989, the gang clashed with Malyshev's Gang, another leading criminal group of Leningrad, in a bloody armed conflict.

descriptions: Tambovskaya Bratva briefly allied with Malyshevskaya OGG to fight the Kazan gang.
edges.evidence_quote: He later briefly allied with Alexander Malyshev, who had controlled the narcotics trade since 1993 after ousting the Azerbaijani mafia from the trade, to fight the Kazan gang from Tatarstan.

descriptions: Infobox lists Solntsevskaya Bratva as a rival of Tambovskaya Bratva.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Solntsevskaya Bratva

descriptions: Tambovskaya Bratva believed Velikie Luki were responsible for an assassination attempt on Kumarin, leading to retaliation.
edges.evidence_quote: He believed that the Velikie Luki were responsible and, later, Viktor Gavrilenkov and Nikolai Gavrilenkov were shot with Viktor Gavrilenkov being killed in the bedlam.

descriptions: Tambov gang fought a bloody skirmish with Velikolukskaya.
edges.evidence_quote: A bloody skirmish with the Velikolukskaya gang caused Kumarin to fall into a month-long coma.

descriptions: Tambov gang initially merged into Velikolukskaya for protection.
edges.evidence_quote: the gang merged into the large criminal group Velikolukskaya

descriptions: The Tambov gang is very closely aligned with Japanese yakuza from Kobe and Osaka.
edges.evidence_quote: The Tambov gang of Saint Petersburg is very closely aligned with ... Japanese yakuza from Kobe and Osaka.

descriptions: Tambovskaya Bratva associates were introduced to Japanese yakuza, indicating a cooperative connection.
edges.evidence_quote: Temo Davidov, a member of the Chechen mafia ... introduced the Russian mafia, Petrov, Kuzmin, and Malyshev, to the son of a Japanese yakuza from Kobe-Osaka, Kinichi Kamiyasu.

New York divorce coercion gang.description: A Haredi Jewish group based in the New York metropolitan area that kidnapped and tortured Jewish men to force them to grant their wives religious divorces. Founded by Rabbi Mendel Epstein in the mid-1980s, the group was broken up by the FBI in October 2013. Three of four prosecuted members were convicted in late 2015.

Takumi-gumi.description: The Takumi-gumi is a Japanese yakuza syndicate based in the Kansai region, founded in 1967 by Masaru Takumi. It was originally part of the Yamaguchi-gumi, with Takumi serving as second-in-command. After Takumi's assassination in 1997, Tadashi Irie became the leader. The group withdrew from the Yamaguchi-gumi in 2015 to form the Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi, and later left that group in 2022. By 2023, membership had dwindled to about 20 members.

descriptions: Irie-gumi merged into Takumi-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: he merged it into the Takumi-gumi

descriptions: Takumi-gumi was a constituent group within Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: The Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi yakuza organization included ... Osaka -based Takumi-gumi

descriptions: Nakano-kai assassinated the head of Takumi-gumi, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Takumi was shot and killed in a coffee shop ... by members of a breakaway Yamaguchi affiliate, the Nakano-kai

descriptions: Takumi-gumi was a founding member of Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi, a splinter group, and later left it.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2015, the Takumi-gumi, along with other yakuza organisations, withdrew from the Yamaguchi-gumi to form the Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi , which withdrew from the Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi in 2022.

descriptions: Takumi-gumi retaliated against Nakano-kai after Takumi's assassination.
edges.evidence_quote: retaliating ruthlessly against the Nakano-kai

descriptions: Members of Nakano-kai assassinated the leader of Takumi-gumi, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Takumi was kumicho of yakuza's 1000-members until his assassination in Kobe in 1997 by members of the Nakano-kai.

descriptions: Takumi-gumi is a sub-organization of Yamaguchi-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: he also headed his own sub-organization, the 1000-member Takumi-gumi

descriptions: 2nd Takumi-gumi is a subsidiary of 6th Yamaguchi-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: the head (kumicho) of the Osaka-based 2nd Takumi-gumi and the grand general manager (so-honbucho) of the 6th Yamaguchi-gumi

descriptions: Takumi-gumi was founded by a former high-ranking member of Yamaguchi-gumi, indicating a structural predecessor relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: The Second Takumi-gumi is a Japanese yakuza syndicate. The Kansai -based yakuza was founded in 1967 by Masaru Takumi , the longtime second-in-command ( wakagashira ) and financial oversteer of the Yamaguchi-gumi.

descriptions: Takumi-gumi broke away from Yamaguchi-gumi in 2015.
edges.evidence_quote: powerful factions, including the Kobe-based Yamaken-gumi, the Osaka-based Takumi-gumi, and the Kyoyu-kai, broke away from the Yamaguchi-gumi and formed a new group called the Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi
